principles: Prefer node-, scene-, resource-, project-setting-, and inspector-based implementation.
Use code only for behavior, physics queries, terrain runtime interaction, combat rules, and networking.
Keep version 0.1 focused on one playable tank type and one reliable local gameplay loop.
Represent tunable gameplay data as Godot Resources so future vehicles can share the same foundation.
Separate MVP requirements from post-MVP systems so experimental features do not weaken the first playable prototype.

version_0_1_mvp.goal: One playable basic tank can drive on terrain, aim, shoot, hit targets, and react correctly to terrain, collision, and line of sight.
version_0_1_mvp.scope_rule: Only one tank type is required. No drones, no spider tanks, no armor deflection, no loadout system, no full multiplayer combat yet.

must_have.title: Vehicle Stats Resource
must_have.description: Create a reusable Godot Resource for tank stats instead of hardcoding movement, health, turret, and terrain values in scripts.
node_first_notes: Assign the stats resource on the Tank scene through the inspector.
Expose materials, masks, speeds, health, and slope values as exported properties or resources.
acceptance: Tank scene has an assigned stats resource.
Movement, turret, health, and weapon values are read from stats.
A designer can tune the basic tank without editing GDScript.

must_have.title: Local Vehicle Test Scene
must_have.description: Create a direct-launch local test scene with terrain, slopes, obstacles, one player tank, projectile targets, and camera.
acceptance: Scene can be launched without Steam lobby.
Scene contains at least one clear line-of-sight target and one blocked line-of-sight case.
Scene contains flat ground and at least one meaningful slope or terrain height change.

must_have.title: Terrain Sampling And Ground Contact
must_have.description: Sample terrain height and normals under the vehicle so driving, slope slowdown, and visual alignment can use the actual terrain surface.
implementation_notes: Start with a small set of sample points under the tank: center, front, rear, left, and right.
Use TerrainWorld as the abstraction point so placeholder terrain and Terrain3D can share the same tank-facing API.
Keep the tank collision stable before adding visual pitch/roll polish.
acceptance: Tank does not fall through terrain.
Tank does not jitter badly on flat ground.
Terrain normal is available to the movement code.
The implementation can later read Terrain3D heights without changing tank gameplay code.

must_have.title: Slope Slowdown
must_have.description: Calculate a speed factor from the slope under the vehicle. Steeper terrain slows the basic tank more strongly.
future_reuse: Spider tanks can use a lower slope_slowdown_strength.
Spider tanks can use a higher max_climb_angle_degrees.
Fast wheeled or light tracked vehicles can have higher top speed but stronger slope penalties.
acceptance: Basic tank noticeably slows on steep terrain.
Basic tank keeps full or near-full speed on flat terrain.
Slope parameters are tunable from the stats resource.

must_have.title: Tank Track Controls
must_have.description: Use WASD as differential track control for the basic tank.
acceptance: Tank can drive forward and reverse.
Tank can pivot and steer like a tracked vehicle.
Control feel is tunable through stats.

must_have.title: Mouse Turret Aiming
must_have.description: Turret rotates independently from the chassis and aims at the mouse ray projected onto terrain.
acceptance: Turret follows mouse aim independently from chassis rotation.
Muzzle position remains correct after turret rotation.
Turret turn speed can be limited by stats.

must_have.title: Aim Line And Obstruction Feedback
must_have.description: Show a line from the muzzle to the mouse target. The line is green when uninterrupted and red when blocked by terrain or props.
acceptance: Aim ray ignores the owning tank.
Aim ray hits terrain and obstacle collision layers.
Green line reaches the aim target when clear.
Red line stops at the obstruction point when blocked.

must_have.title: Collision Layer Foundation
must_have.description: Define and document collision layers for tank body, terrain, props, projectiles, hitboxes, and aim ray queries.
acceptance: Tank movement collision is separate from aim ray collision where needed.
Projectile collision can detect terrain, props, and tank hitboxes.
Self-collision is ignored by own aim and own freshly spawned projectile.

must_have.title: Shooting Foundation
must_have.description: Fire a projectile from the muzzle using the turret direction. Version 0.1 only needs local play and one weapon.
requirements: Projectile scene has visible mesh, collision shape, speed, lifetime, damage, and impact signal.
Tank uses fire cooldown from stats.
Projectile starts at Muzzle and travels along turret forward direction.
Projectile does not instantly hit the firing tank.
acceptance: Left mouse fires one projectile when cooldown allows.
Projectile is visible and travels in the turret direction.
Projectile despawns on impact or lifetime timeout.

must_have.title: Hit Detection And Damage
must_have.description: Detect projectile impacts against tank hitboxes, terrain, and props. Apply simple direct damage for version 0.1.
requirements: Basic tank has simple hitbox or collision body suitable for projectile hits.
Projectile impact reports hit position, normal, target node, and damage value.
Health is reduced on direct hit.
Impact feedback is visible enough for testing.
acceptance: Projectile can hit a target tank or dummy target.
Hit target health changes.
Projectile impact against terrain or obstacle stops the projectile.
Hit information is structured so armor systems can be added later.

must_have.title: Gameplay Feedback
must_have.description: Add minimal feedback for driving, aiming, shooting, and hits without polishing final VFX.
acceptance: Aim line clearly communicates clear or blocked shot.
Shot origin and direction are readable.
Hit impact is visible.
Health or damage debug display is available in the test scene.

go_criteria: Basic tank drives reliably on the test scene terrain.
Slope slowdown works and is tunable.
Turret aiming and green/red line-of-sight feedback work.
Projectile firing works from the turret muzzle.
Projectiles hit terrain, props, and a target hitbox correctly.
A hit reduces target health in local play.
Godot script checks and scene boot checks pass.

version_0_2_multiplayer_foundation.goal: Move the version 0.1 local tank loop into the Steam lobby match flow.
features: Spawn one basic tank per Steam peer.
Clients send input and aim intent.
Host synchronizes tank position, chassis rotation, turret rotation, shot spawn, and hit events.
Lobby remains the entry point with host, join, copy, paste, ping, map preview, and start match.

version_0_3_terrain_damage.goal: Add Terrain3D deformation and make movement react to changed terrain.
features: Projectile impact can create a crater event.
Crater event changes visible terrain height.
Terrain collision refresh happens after crater changes.
Tank terrain sampling sees updated crater heights.
Host sends reliable crater events to clients.
Late joiners can replay crater event history.

version_0_4_armor_and_projectile_rules.goal: Introduce directional armor and deflection after basic shooting and hits are already reliable.
features: Tank gets separate hit zones: front, side, rear, turret, tracks.
Projectile impact stores hit position, impact normal, shot direction, and target local position.
Armor stats define thickness or resistance per hit zone.
Deflection chance or penetration result is calculated from impact angle and armor zone.
Non-penetrating hits can ricochet, spark, or apply reduced damage.
Track hits can later cause mobility damage without destroying the tank.
version_0_4_armor_and_projectile_rules.important_note: Do not start this before version 0.1 hit detection is deterministic and easy to debug.

version_0_5_vehicle_variants.goal: Use the shared stats system to add meaningful vehicle variety.

features.name: Fast Tank
features.tradeoff: Higher top speed, weaker armor, stronger slowdown on steep terrain.

features.name: Heavy Tank
features.tradeoff: More health and armor, lower acceleration, slower turret rotation.

features.name: Spider Tank
features.tradeoff: Lower top speed, minimal slope slowdown, higher climb angle, different movement animation later.

version_0_6_drones_and_countermeasures.goal: Add drone systems only after tanks, terrain, combat, and networking have stable foundations.
version_0_6_drones_and_countermeasures.design_rule: Drones and countermeasures should use modular stats and loadout resources, not special-case code.

immediate_next_steps: Create VehicleStats resource for the basic tank.
Move current tank tuning values into the stats resource.
Create a local VehicleTest scene with terrain, obstacles, target dummy, and camera.
Implement terrain normal sampling behind TerrainWorld API.
Apply slope slowdown to tank movement.
Implement direct hit detection and basic health damage.
